HackerOne's Internet Bug Bounty (IBB) paused new submissions as of March 27, 2026.

IBB rewards vulnerability discovery and remediation in open source projects like Django, Rails, and Apache.

AI is expanding vuln discovery faster than the open source ecosystem can remediate.

The proof is in the Percolator.

Not only are we building in public -> we are building completely open source.

726 commits + 500+ deployments + 11 forks. External contributors getting paid for PR's and bug reports.

Permissionless perpetual futures -> deploy a perp market for

$30,000 Biggest MEGA AI Hackathon

Build RL environments on Meta's OpenEnv 

~ Interview access at Meta & Hugging Face
~ Free RL training from Hugging Face & PyTorch
~ Your code ships to a Meta open source project

Registration closes in 3 HOURS

every model has the ability to reveal zero days in commonly used open source projects

(humans do too) 
I have seen bug bounty hackers find CVE 8+ 0-days on next.js 2-3 times 

I have seen gemini pro find 0-day SQLis in other OSS stuff 

that future is already here

4 Hackathons.. 4 Weeks.. $4,000..

REGISTRATION IS NOW OPEN ON 
@devfolio
!

The Locus Paygentic Hackathon Series spans 4 weeks! 1 track per week, $4000 in prizes over the series. 

Sign up at the devfolio link below and join the discord!
paygentic-week1.devfolio.co/overview

BGA Spring 2026 Incubation is open. 

If you are building with blockchain to solve something real, this is your window. Applications close 13 March.

 Grants of up to $10,000 USDT

 1 on 1

Did you know that Blink Labs has had a paid open source contributor program for a year? We now have 10 contributors and counting. Our most recent addition joined us by contributing to our projects consistently. Now they're getting a paycheck. No milestones. No reports. Just code.

Axel 
@Axel_bitblaze69
 built a viral open-source tool for Claude AI called TradingView MCP.

It’s already blowing up and even has its own page on X trending right now 



Supporting Alex with 
@Pumpfun
 GitHub Sponsorship since he needs sponsors for this

Love the work Web3 Foundation is doing with grants. We help foundations track the real on-chain impact of funded projects by filtering out sybil noise from actual user growth. Could be a game-changer for your next funding cycle. Open to a brief sync?

Most early Web3 founders don’t get access to top advisors.
Web3 X founders do.

Learn from operators from:
L1/L2 ecosystems
VC funds
Exchanges
Security firms
Infra projects

This is the network most startups get after funding.
We give you access earlier.

Apply

TiE Silicon Valley’s Funding Fridays connects founders with investors through curated 1:1 meetings to support their next funding round.

This month’s participating VC: Venky Karnam, General Partner at Shift Left Ventures.

Apply: 
luma.com/xom46wqq

#FundingFridays

Tips for Founders:
 Apply early for grants — they help validate your idea before big VC money. 
 Use hackathons to prototype fast and get real feedback. 
 Read evaluation guidelines (like why your project fits Solana). 
 Join developer networks — it makes growth faster.

Open Source Intel op X: 'Iranian reports say the bounty for the remaining U.S. pilot has been raised to 15 billion tomans (~$90,000), with a Khuzestan company offering the reward for information on his location.'

Yeh, I appreciate that - definitely not blaming them. They also get a lot more AI reports due to the nature of open-source, but it's not gonna stop attackers. I think a lot of bug bounty programs will move more towards private programs with trusted researchers.

AI is finding vulnerabilities faster than humans can fix them. Curl had to shut down its bug bounty -- drowning in AI-generated reports. Now 7 companies including Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI just pledged $12.5M to defend open source.

Why it’s worth checking out 
•  Perfect for developers & open-source contributors: Turn GitHub issues into paid bounties
•  Great for freelancers: Find real tasks in Web3, content, design, social, and more
